# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Deployment Manager template for Google Compute Engine instances. Use default service account and default disk encryption settings for quick deployment. Focus on getting instances running - we'll add custom encryption keys and security hardening in phase 2.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent cloud compute security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

# Google Compute Engine Deployment Manager Template

# compute_instance.py - Jinja2/Python template for GCE instances

resources:
- name: secure-vm-instance
  type: compute.v1.instance
  properties:
    zone: us-central1-a
    machineType: zones/us-central1-a/machineTypes/n2-standard-2
    
    # Disable external IP to reduce attack surface - use Cloud NAT/IAP for access
    networkInterfaces:
    - network: global/networks/default
      subnetwork: regions/us-central1/subnetworks/default
      # No accessConfigs means no external IP - enforcing least privilege network access
    
    # Use Customer-Managed Encryption Keys (CMEK) for disk encryption
    disks:
    - deviceName: boot-disk
      type: PERSISTENT
      boot: true
      autoDelete: true
      initializeParams:
        sourceImage: projects/cos-cloud/global/images/family/cos-stable
        diskSizeGb: 50
        diskType: zones/us-central1-a/diskTypes/pd-ssd
      diskEncryptionKey:
        kmsKeyName: projects/my-project/locations/us-central1/keyRings/compute-keyring/cryptoKeys/disk-encryption-key
    
    # Use a dedicated service account with minimal permissions (least privilege)
    serviceAccounts:
    - email: compute-minimal-sa@my-project.iam.gserviceaccount.com
      scopes:
      - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/logging.write
      - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/monitoring.write
    
    # Security hardening metadata
    metadata:
      items:
      # Disable legacy metadata endpoint (v0.1/v1beta1) to prevent SSRF-based credential theft
      - key: disable-legacy-endpoints
        value: 'true'
      # Block project-wide SSH keys - only instance-level keys allowed
      - key: block-project-ssh-keys
        value: 'true'
      # Enable OS Login for IAM-based SSH access instead of metadata SSH keys
      - key: enable-oslogin
        value: 'TRUE'
      # Enable integrity monitoring for Shielded VM
      - key: enable-integrity-monitoring
        value: 'true'
    
    # Shielded VM settings - protect against rootkits and bootkits
    shieldedInstanceConfig:
      enableSecureBoot: true
      enableVtpm: true
      enableIntegrityMonitoring: true
    
    # Confidential computing for memory encryption (if supported by machine type)
    # confidentialInstanceConfig:
    #   enableConfidentialCompute: true
    
    # Apply network tags for firewall rule targeting (allowlist approach)
    tags:
      items:
      - secure-instance
      - no-external-access
    
    # Labels for resource management and audit
    labels:
      environment: production
      security-tier: hardened
      managed-by: deployment-manager

# Firewall rule - explicit allowlist for internal traffic only
- name: allow-internal-only
  type: compute.v1.firewall
  properties:
    network: global/networks/default
    priority: 1000
    direction: INGRESS
    # Only allow traffic from known internal CIDR ranges
    sourceRanges:
    - 10.128.0.0/9
    # Only allow specific ports needed by the application
    allowed:
    - IPProtocol: tcp
      ports:
      - '443'
    targetTags:
    - secure-instance

# Deny all other ingress by default (defense in depth)
- name: deny-all-ingress
  type: compute.v1.firewall
  properties:
    network: global/networks/default
    priority: 65534
    direction: INGRESS
    sourceRanges:
    - 0.0.0.0/0
    denied:
    - IPProtocol: tcp
    - IPProtocol: udp
    - IPProtocol: icmp
    targetTags:
    - secure-instance

# IAP tunnel firewall rule - allow SSH only through Identity-Aware Proxy
- name: allow-iap-ssh
  type: compute.v1.firewall
  properties:
    network: global/networks/default
    priority: 900
    direction: INGRESS
    # Google's IAP IP range
    sourceRanges:
    - 35.235.240.0/20
    allowed:
    - IPProtocol: tcp
      ports:
      - '22'
    targetTags:
    - secure-instance

outputs:
- name: instanceName
  value: $(ref.secure-vm-instance.name)
- name: internalIP
  value: $(ref.secure-vm-instance.networkInterfaces[0].networkIP)
# Note: No external IP output since we intentionally omit external access
